axis_responsibility.Agent: Owns Agent Object adapter_refs and optional preferred_adapter_ref as capability metadata only.
axis_responsibility.Brick: Owns preset step declarations, Building Plan step rows, required return shapes, and fleet route shape.
axis_responsibility.Link: Owns gate sufficiency, fan-in frontier observation, Movement, and transition facts; no new Link vocabulary is needed.
axis_responsibility.Support: Reads declared Brick/Agent data, stamps it into plans, validates shape/capability, and records evidence without choosing route or judging quality.

checker_or_verifier_plan: Extend existing `materialize_building_intent_case` assertions to check ordered per-step adapter/model stamps for linear and graph presets.
Add FIRE cases: materializer ignores step adapter -> RED; per-step adapter not declared by lane Agent Object -> loud reject; preferred_adapter_ref outside adapter_refs -> loud reject.
Add fleet preset cases asserting graph shape, fan-out/fan-in groups, strict-evidence gate placement, fan-in-wait-all frontier concept, and no Link vocabulary additions.
If repeated lanes are admitted, add positive explicit-repeat and negative missing-repeat-key cases.
Run focused profile in-tree only if it writes nothing; run full `check_profile.py --all` only in a temp source copy outside the repo.
returned.design_summary: Open the builder-side provider ladder by resolving provider selection per materialized step as declared data: step declaration > Agent Object preferred_adapter_ref > building default. Stamp the resolved adapter/model onto the declared step only when it differs from or must override plan-level fallback, and validate the selected adapter against the resolved Agent Object adapter_refs before the plan can run.
edge_cases: A top-level provider-specific model with a different lane-preferred adapter should reject unless the step declares a compatible model or uses `model:default`.
A per-step adapter for a lane that does not declare that adapter must reject before run.
`preferred_adapter_ref` outside `adapter_refs` must reject at Agent resource resolution.
Current graph materializer derives node ids from step-template kind; repeated same-kind fleet lanes need an explicit unique lane key or they collide.
The AGENTS-referenced current operating packet paths are absent in this checkout, so packet confirmation is not proven.
invariants: Step selected_adapter_ref overrides Agent preference and building default.
Agent preferred_adapter_ref overrides building default only when no step adapter is declared.
Every final selected_adapter_ref must be declared by the resolved Agent Object adapter_refs.
selected_model_ref must either be `model:default` or match the selected adapter provider grammar.
A provider/model stamp is selection data only; it is not provider login, model availability, source truth, success, quality, or Movement authority.
not_proven: No checker command was run in this read-only design step.
Full gate green in a temp source copy is not proven here.
The untracked fleet preset files are observed local candidates, not admitted source truth.
Provider availability, provider quality, and model availability are not proven.
Runtime parallel execution is not proven or required.
Source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, and Movement authority are not claimed.
observed_evidence: Repository inspection was read-only; `git status --short` shows untracked `brick/templates/presets/recon-fleet.md` and `brick/templates/presets/review-fleet.md` already present.
Engine admission already reads `selected_adapter_ref` and `selected_model_ref` from step first, then plan fallback.

Compact builder materialization currently stamps only building-wide adapter/model; linear `_materializer_declared_step` returns no step-level adapter/model, and graph `_materializer_graph_node` does not copy raw preset step adapter/model into nodes.
Direct declared step-template rendering already has a step-level adapter/model carry seam, so the repair can reuse the existing declared-plan shape.
Agent Object schema currently rejects unknown keys, so `preferred_adapter_ref` requires resolver/checker admission before adding it to `agent/objects/*.yaml`.
returned.output_excerpt: { "observed_evidence": [ "Repository inspection was read-only; `git status --short` shows untracked `brick/templates/presets/recon-fleet.md` and `brick/templates/presets/review-fleet.md` already present.", "Engine admission already reads `selected_adapter_ref` and `selected_model_ref` from step first, then plan fallback.", "Compact builder materialization currently stamps only building-wide adapter/model; linear `_materializer_declared_step` returns no step-level adapter/model, and graph `_materializer_graph_node` does not copy raw preset step adapter/model into nodes.", "Direct declared st...
proposed_changes: Add a support helper that resolves final step adapter/model from raw preset step, resolved Agent Object, and building defaults; reject when the final adapter is not in that Agent Object's `adapter_refs`.
Use that helper in linear preset materialization, graph preset materialization, and direct graph composition so per-step declarations cannot be dropped before plan validation.
Admit optional `preferred_adapter_ref` in `support/connection/agent_resources.py`; require it to be one clean adapter ref already present in the same Agent Object `adapter_refs`.
For fleet presets, admit `recon-fleet` and `review-fleet` as common graph presets; keep the Claude provider example as a step-level review lens only.

If true repeated N lanes of the same step template are required, add an explicit preset step lane key such as `step_instance_ref` for unique node/step refs; otherwise keep current fleet lenses on distinct reviewer templates.
relevant_current_structure: Brick presets live in `brick/templates/presets/*.md` frontmatter and are loaded verbatim into the chain preset registry.
Graph fleet presets are already supported structurally through `target_word` fan-out/fan-in markers, graph groups, and `fan-in-wait-all` frontier observation.
Link gate vocabulary already maps `strict-evidence` to `link-gate:strict`; `fan-in-wait-all` is not a GateFact literal.
Run admission still enforces that selected adapter must be referenced by the Agent Object.
Model refs are adapter-scoped grammar and provider availability remains outside proof.
returned.returned_summary: local CLI Agent Adapter returned support evidence
unchanged_surfaces: No `link/` edit, no new Movement literal, no new GateFact literal, and no route target invention.
No scheduler, queue, retry runtime, provider runtime, or runtime parallelism claim.
No write-capability law change: effective write remains Brick write_scope need plus Agent policy plus adapter capability plus write observation.
No model-specific Agent identity and no provider availability proof.
Checkers and materializers remain support evidence only, not source truth or quality judgment.
